content:
**Summary of the Finnish Ministry of Defence Press Release:**

On Sunday morning, several slow-moving, low-flying small objects were detected in Finnish airspace over sea areas and Southeast Finland. The Air Force deployed an F/A-18 Hornet fighter jet to identify the objects.

Two drones were confirmed to have crashed—one north of Kouvola and another east of Kouvola. Police have secured the crash sites for further investigation.

Defence Minister Antti Häkkänen stated that the drones had entered Finnish territory and emphasized that authorities are treating the incident with seriousness, noting that security forces responded immediately.

The Defence Forces, in coordination with other authorities, are investigating the incident.
